content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_001_page_001.jpg

1. Paulos, apostle of Christos Yeshua through [the] will of God, and Timotheos the brother, to the assembly of God, the [one] being in Korinthos, with all the holy [ones] being in the whole of Achaia:

2. Grace to you and peace from God [the] Father of us and [the] Lord Yeshua Christos.

3. Blessed [be] the God and Father of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos, the Father of the mercies and God of all comfort,

4. the [one] comforting us in all the affliction of us, so that we are able to comfort the [ones] in every affliction through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.

5. Because just as the sufferings of the Christos abound to us, so also through the Christos abounds the comfort of us.

6. And whether we are afflicted, [it is] for the comfort and salvation of you; or whether we are comforted, [it is] for the comfort of you, the [one] working in [the] endurance of the same sufferings which we also suffer.

7. And the hope of us [is] firm for you, knowing that as you are sharers of the sufferings, so also [you are sharers] of the comfort.

8. For we do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning the affliction of us, the [one] having come to us in Asia, that beyond measure, beyond power, we were burdened, so that we despaired even of the living.

9. But we ourselves have had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we might not trust in ourselves but in God, the [one] raising the dead,

10. who from so great a death delivered us and will deliver — in whom we have hoped that he will also still deliver —

11. you also helping together in prayer for us, that from many faces the gift [given] to us through many may be given thanks for on behalf of us.

12. For the boast of us is this: the testimony of the conscience of us, that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in [the] grace of God, we conducted ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.

13. For we write nothing else to you than what you read or also understand. And I hope that you will understand until [the] end,

14. just as you also understood us in part, that we are the boast of you just as you also [are] ours in the day of the Lord Yeshua.

15. And in this confidence I was intending to come to you first, that you might have a second grace,

16. and through you to pass into Makedonia, and again from Makedonia to come to you, and by you to be sent on [my way] to Ioudaia.

17. Therefore intending this, did I then use fickleness? Or the [things] I intend, do I intend according to flesh, that there should be with me the "yes, yes" and the "no, no"?

18. But faithful [is] God, that the word of us to you is not "yes" and "no."

19. For the Son of God, Yeshua Christos, the [one] having been proclaimed among you through us — through me and Silvanos and Timotheos — was not "yes" and "no," but in him "yes" has come to be.

20. For as many as [are the] promises of God, in him [is] the "yes." Therefore also through him [is] the "Amen" to God for glory through us.

21. And the [one] confirming us with you into Christos and having anointed us [is] God,

22. the [one] also having sealed us and having given the guarantee of the Spirit in the hearts of us.

23. And I call upon God as witness upon the soul of me, that sparing you I did not yet come to Korinthos.

24. Not that we lord over the faith of you, but we are fellow workers of the joy of you; for by the faith you stand.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "Father of mercies and God of all comfort":** παράκλησις ("comfort/encouragement") appears 10 times in vv.3-7. The root παρακαλ- dominates this opening — comfort is the theological key to the entire letter.

2. **v.8 — "the affliction in Asia":** Paul refers to a near-death experience in Ephesus (cf. Acts 19:23-41?). The severity is extreme: "beyond measure, beyond power...despaired even of living."

3. **v.9 — "sentence of death":** ἀπόκριμα τοῦ θανάτου — a technical legal term for a death verdict. Paul experienced this crisis as providential: it taught him to rely on the God who raises the dead.

4. **v.19 — Silvanus:** Σιλουανός — likely the same as Silas of Acts 15-18. He co-founded the Corinthian church with Paul and Timothy.

5. **v.20 — "In him is the Yes":** All God's promises find their affirmation in Christ. The "Amen" spoken in worship is grounded in Christ's fulfillment of every divine promise.

6. **v.22 — "seal" and "guarantee":** σφραγισάμενος (sealed) and ἀρραβών (guarantee/down payment) — commercial metaphors. The Spirit is both the mark of ownership and the first installment of the full inheritance to come.

— EMET, AI Translator
